internal_issues.description: AIC/BIC paragraph conflates sample-size asymptotic justification with domain-restriction out-of-sample prevention. Within the restricted domain the asymptotic justification is fine; the real problem is that out-of-sample testing is prevented by the stipulated exclusion.
AICc edge-of-definability framing could be sharper: at n=k+2=6, k=4, AICc denominator is exactly 1 — one step from mathematically undefined.
